journal_entry.description: Capitalization of land improvements - fencing, streetlights, walkways, and landscaping
journal_entry.rationale: Under IFRS, costs that enhance the utility of land and have finite useful lives are capitalized as Land Improvements (a depreciable asset separate from Land). Fencing, streetlights, walkways, and permanent landscaping meet this criterion. HST of 13% is recoverable on these capital expenditures in Ontario.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Fencing, streetlights, walkways, and permanent landscaping are land improvements with finite lives, capitalized as a depreciable asset. Total base cost $4,110,000 ($1,750,000 + $2,360,000) plus 13% HST. Cash paid for first portion, cheque (bank) for second. One asset increase (Land Improvements), two asset decreases (Cash and Bank).
